name: Media gallery

sections.note: One or more sections, each with optional headline and media tiles

selection_mode.note: Activated by checking a tile checkbox. Changes click behaviour from open to select.

headline.note: Reflects the grouping criterion e.g. date, media type

layout.columns_below_584px: Minimum tile width 128px — columns calculated from available width
layout.columns_above_1200px: Minimum tile width 282px — columns calculated from available width

selection_mode_behaviour.default_click: Opens media preview or detail view
selection_mode_behaviour.activation: Ticking any tile checkbox activates selection mode for all tiles
selection_mode_behaviour.selection_click: In selection mode, clicking the whole tile selects/deselects it
selection_mode_behaviour.action_bar: Universal action bar actions are replaced by selection-specific actions

usage_rules.detail: Use media gallery only for collections of graphical objects such as patient images. Not for action lists or non-visual content.
All media tiles in a gallery must either all show details (sublabel, actions) or none show them. Do not mix.
If sections are used, the headline of each section must reflect the grouping criterion (e.g. the date, the media type). Do not use generic or unrelated headlines.
Always provide a useful default grouping and sort order, e.g. most recent first on a patient detail page.
Selection mode activates for all tiles when any tile checkbox is ticked. It is not per-tile.

confidence_blockers: If ticket mixes tiles with and without details in the same gallery, flag.
If ticket uses gallery for non-image content, flag and suggest List instead.
If ticket adds section headlines that do not reflect a grouping criterion, flag.

embedding_text: Media gallery component. Grid of media tiles for graphical objects patient images. Responsive column layout fixed gutter. Optional section headlines for grouping by date or type. Selection mode activates on checkbox tick changes click to select. All tiles consistent detail display. Skeleton loading state.
